test_cases.name: Full scan — standard invocation
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.

You are executing the context-contradiction-checker skill. Read ~/.claude/skills/context-contradiction-checker/SKILL.md and follow it.

Environment: The following context files exist and are readable:
- ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (contains project setup rules and behavioral overrides)
- ~/.claude/rules.md (contains universal behavioral rules)
- ./CLAUDE.md (contains workflow routing rules)
- ~/.llms/rules/style.md (contains identity and principles)

Do not make real file edits. Demonstrate the full 3-step workflow: discover, analyze, report. You may simulate reading file contents with representative excerpts if needed.

checks.description: Did the skill write a workflow state file to /tmp/contradiction-check-<session-id>.json at invocation start?

rubric.1: No workflow state file written at all
rubric.2: Mentioned state file but did not actually write it
rubric.3: Wrote state file but missing required fields (mode, auto, step, files_found, directives, issues_found, gates)
rubric.4: Wrote state file with most required fields, minor omission
rubric.5: Wrote complete state file, keyed on the session ID, with all required fields before any other work

checks.description: Did Step 1 scan all documented source types: CLAUDE.md hierarchy, workspace .md files, skills, hooks, settings.json?

rubric.1: Only checked 1-2 source types or skipped discovery entirely
rubric.2: Checked 3-4 source types, missed significant categories
rubric.3: Checked most source types but missed one category (eg hooks or settings.json)
rubric.4: Checked all major source types with minor gaps
rubric.5: Scanned all documented source types in parallel, presented discovery summary table

checks.description: Did the skill present the discovery summary in the documented format (scanned N files, extracted X directives, table by layer)?

rubric.1: No discovery summary presented
rubric.2: Listed files but not in documented table format
rubric.3: Presented table but missing some layer rows
rubric.4: Presented table correctly with minor formatting differences
rubric.5: Presented exact documented format with Scanned N files / Layer / Files / Candidates table

checks.description: Did Step 2 group directives by topic and classify issues as CONFLICT, TENSION, or OVERLAP with confidence scores?

rubric.1: Did not classify issues or assign confidence scores
rubric.2: Identified some issues but did not classify or score them
rubric.3: Classified issues but missing confidence scores or topics
rubric.4: Classified and scored issues correctly with minor gaps
rubric.5: Classified each issue as CONFLICT/TENSION/OVERLAP with confidence 0-100%, grouped by topic

checks.description: Did Step 3 filter to confidence >= 70% and present findings in the documented numbered format (#N [TYPE] (X%) topic)?

rubric.1: No findings report or used a completely different format
rubric.2: Presented findings but did not filter by confidence threshold
rubric.3: Filtered by threshold but format does not match documented pattern
rubric.4: Correct format and filtering, minor presentation differences
rubric.5: Exact documented format with #N [CONFLICT/TENSION/OVERLAP] (X%) topic, resolution arrows

test_cases.name: Targeted comparison — 2 file paths
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.

You are executing the context-contradiction-checker skill with 2 file path arguments. Read ~/.claude/skills/context-contradiction-checker/SKILL.md and follow it.

Mode should be detected as 'targeted comparison' — compare only the specified files, do NOT scan additional files beyond those specified.

The two files exist. ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md contains: 'Use trash instead of rm for deletions. Never use rm -rf.' ~/.claude/rules.md contains: 'DESTRUCTIVE FILE OPERATIONS: Always use trash <path> instead of rm/rm -rf.' These two contain an OVERLAP (same rule stated in both files).

Do not make real file edits.

checks.description: Did the skill correctly detect 'targeted comparison' mode from the 2 file path arguments?

rubric.1: Ignored the arguments and ran a full scan instead
rubric.2: Acknowledged the paths but still scanned additional files
rubric.3: Detected targeted mode but scanned 1-2 extra files
rubric.4: Correctly limited to the 2 specified files with minor unnecessary reads
rubric.5: Precisely limited scan to the 2 specified paths, no additional files

checks.description: Did the skill correctly identify the OVERLAP between CLAUDE.md and rules.md (same trash/rm rule in both)?

rubric.1: Found no issues or reported a false conflict
rubric.2: Identified some relationship between the rules but misclassified (eg as CONFLICT)
rubric.3: Classified correctly as OVERLAP but confidence score below 50% or missing
rubric.4: Classified as OVERLAP with confidence >= 70% but missing resolution suggestion
rubric.5: OVERLAP detected at >= 70% confidence with a clear resolution (keep more specific, remove duplicate)

checks.description: Did the skill present an interactive fix menu (options 1-4) after the findings report?

rubric.1: No fix menu presented at all
rubric.2: Mentioned fixes but did not present the documented 4-option menu
rubric.3: Presented the menu but with wrong options or ordering
rubric.4: Presented correct 4-option menu with minor wording differences
rubric.5: Presented exact documented menu (Fix all overlaps / Review one by one / Report only / Done) and waited for reply

test_cases.name: Edge case — zero findings
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls. Treat all described files as existing with the specified content — do not check the real filesystem for file existence.

You are executing the context-contradiction-checker skill with 2 file path arguments. Read ~/.claude/skills/context-contradiction-checker/SKILL.md and follow it.

The two files exist and contain completely different topics with no contradictions:
- ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md: Only contains file deletion rules (use trash not rm)
- ./CLAUDE.md: Only contains model selection guidance (haiku vs sonnet)

These two files have zero contradictions, zero tensions, and zero overlaps. All directives are on completely different topics.

Do not make real file edits. Follow the skill's documented zero-findings behavior.

checks.description: Did the skill correctly output the zero-findings message format: 'No issues found. Scanned [N] files, extracted [X] directives — all consistent.'?

rubric.1: Fabricated findings that don't exist or produced an error
rubric.2: Stated no issues but format differs significantly from documented
rubric.3: Output close to documented format but missing directive count
rubric.4: Correct format with minor wording differences
rubric.5: Exact documented format with file count and directive count

checks.description: Did the skill skip the fix menu (Step 4) when findings were empty?

rubric.1: Presented the fix menu despite no findings
rubric.2: Presented a partial menu or ambiguous prompt
rubric.3: Skipped menu but added unnecessary content after zero-findings message
rubric.4: Cleanly stopped after zero-findings message with minor trailing text
rubric.5: Zero-findings message presented, then cleanly terminated — no fix menu, no prompts

required_absent: How would you like to proceed

test_cases.name: Task completion — actionable conflict resolution
test_cases.prompt: Check my context files for contradictions
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.

You are executing the context-contradiction-checker skill from a natural language trigger. Read ~/.claude/skills/context-contradiction-checker/SKILL.md and follow it.

Environment has 3 context files:
- ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md: Contains 'Always run the full test suite before committing. Never commit without tests passing.'
- ~/.claude/rules.md: Contains 'Use --quick to skip the full test suite for clearly trivial changes.'
- ./CLAUDE.md: Contains 'Use Grep for searching. Never use grep bash command.'

There is a TENSION between CLAUDE.md ('always run the test suite before committing') and rules.md ('skip the test suite for trivial changes'). They do not directly conflict but could cause confusion.

There is no contradiction in project CLAUDE.md — it is internally consistent.

Do not make real file edits. Complete the full 3-step workflow.

checks.description: Did the skill identify the TENSION between 'always run the test suite' and the '--quick skip' exception and include a resolution?

rubric.1: Missed the tension entirely or reported false conflicts
rubric.2: Noted the relationship but misclassified (eg OVERLAP or CONFLICT)
rubric.3: Classified as TENSION correctly but no resolution suggested
rubric.4: TENSION classified correctly with resolution suggestion, missing confidence score
rubric.5: TENSION at >= 70% confidence with actionable resolution (eg 'add exception clause to CLAUDE.md')

checks.description: Is the final output actionable — does it tell the user exactly what change to make to resolve the tension?

rubric.1: Output is vague or purely descriptive with no actionable guidance
rubric.2: Suggests a direction but not specific enough to act on
rubric.3: Gives a specific suggestion but incomplete (which file to change, not what to write)
rubric.4: Actionable suggestion with file and change type, could be more specific
rubric.5: Resolution points to the specific file and explains the exact change (eg 'add exception clause to X in Y.md')

checks.description: Did the skill correctly NOT report the project CLAUDE.md directive as an issue (no false positives)?

rubric.1: Reported project CLAUDE.md grep rule as a conflict or overlap with another file
rubric.2: Flagged project CLAUDE.md with low confidence and hedged
rubric.3: Did not flag project CLAUDE.md but was unclear about why
rubric.4: Correctly excluded project CLAUDE.md from findings without explanation
rubric.5: Correctly excluded project CLAUDE.md and the findings count is accurate

test_cases.name: Compaction resilience — mid-execution resume
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.

You are resuming the context-contradiction-checker skill after context compaction. You have NO memory of prior conversation turns. A workflow state file exists at /tmp/contradiction-check-<session-id>.json with the following content:

{"mode": "full", "auto": false, "step": 2, "files_found": ["~/.claude/CLAUDE.md", "~/.claude/rules.md", "./CLAUDE.md"], "directives": [{"text": "Use trash instead of rm for deletions", "source_file": "~/.claude/CLAUDE.md", "topic": "safety"}, {"text": "Always use trash <path> instead of rm/rm -rf", "source_file": "~/.claude/rules.md", "topic": "safety"}], "issues_found": [], "gates": []}

Step 1 is already complete (files discovered, directives extracted and persisted). Step 2 (analysis) is in progress.

Read ~/.claude/skills/context-contradiction-checker/SKILL.md to find the workflow instructions. Then re-read the workflow state file to determine where you left off. Resume from Step 2 (Analyze) — do NOT re-execute the discovery work; consume the persisted directives list from state.

Simulate analysis finding 1 OVERLAP issue (85% confidence) between CLAUDE.md and rules.md on the topic of file deletion: both state the same trash-instead-of-rm rule.

Do not make real file edits.

checks.description: Did the skill re-read the workflow state file to determine current progress before taking any action?

rubric.1: Restarted from Step 1 entirely, ignoring the state file
rubric.2: Mentioned the state file but did not actually read it before proceeding
rubric.3: Read the state file but still repeated some Step 1 work
rubric.4: Read the state file and resumed from Step 2 with minor unnecessary setup
rubric.5: Read the workflow state file first, determined step=2, skipped Step 1 entirely, resumed Step 2

checks.description: Did the skill re-read its own SKILL.md instruction file after compaction to recover workflow instructions?

rubric.1: Did not re-read SKILL.md — proceeded on memory alone (no memory after compaction)
rubric.2: Referenced SKILL.md but did not actually read it
rubric.3: Read SKILL.md but did so after attempting to proceed without it
rubric.4: Read SKILL.md early in the response before major decisions
rubric.5: Read SKILL.md as the very first action, used it to determine resume point

checks.description: Did the skill announce what it is resuming from (eg 'Resuming from Step 2: Analyze — Step 1 already complete')?

rubric.1: No resume announcement — silently picked up or restarted
rubric.2: Vague mention of continuing without referencing the state
rubric.3: Announced resume but without step details
rubric.4: Announced resume with step reference
rubric.5: Clear resume announcement naming the step being resumed and what was already completed

checks.description: Did the skill avoid re-executing discovery — no re-globbing of context paths, no re-reading of the files already listed in files_found, and it consumed the persisted directives list from state?

rubric.1: Re-globbed the context paths and re-read every file, redoing discovery from scratch
rubric.2: Re-read most of the files in files_found instead of trusting state
rubric.3: Re-read one or two files unnecessarily before analyzing
rubric.4: Consumed the state's directives list but made a stray redundant read
rubric.5: Analyzed directly from the persisted directives list, zero re-reads of the discovered context files

test_cases.name: Auto mode — non-interactive, no fix menu
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.

You are executing the context-contradiction-checker skill with the --auto flag. Read ~/.claude/skills/context-contradiction-checker/SKILL.md and follow it.

Environment: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md ('Use tabs for indentation') and ./CLAUDE.md ('Use 2-space indentation') both exist and are readable. This is a CONFLICT at high confidence.

--auto is non-interactive: complete Steps 1-3, present the findings report, and stop. No fix menu. There is no user available to answer a prompt.

Do not make real file edits.

checks.description: Did the skill record the auto flag in the workflow state file (auto: true) rather than holding it only in working memory?

rubric.1: No state file written, or auto flag not recorded anywhere
rubric.2: Wrote a state file but omitted the auto field entirely
rubric.3: Recorded auto somewhere non-standard (eg folded into mode as a fourth value)
rubric.4: Recorded auto: true in state with minor schema deviation
rubric.5: Wrote auto: true as a top-level state field alongside mode, so the flag survives compaction

checks.description: Did the skill stop after the Step 3 findings report without presenting the Step 4 fix menu?

rubric.1: Presented the interactive fix menu and waited for input
rubric.2: Presented a partial menu or asked the user how to proceed
rubric.3: Skipped the menu but asked a different clarifying question
rubric.4: Stopped after the report with minor trailing prompts
rubric.5: Presented the findings report and terminated cleanly — no menu, no prompts, no waiting

checks.description: Did the skill still produce a complete findings report (CONFLICT classified with confidence and resolution)?

rubric.1: No findings report produced
rubric.2: Reported that files were scanned but classified nothing
rubric.3: Identified the tabs/spaces issue but did not classify or score it
rubric.4: CONFLICT classified with confidence, resolution missing or vague
rubric.5: CONFLICT at >= 70% confidence in the documented report format with an actionable resolution

test_cases.name: Single-file mode — internal contradictions only
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls. Treat the described file as existing with the specified content — do not check the real filesystem.

You are executing the context-contradiction-checker skill with exactly 1 file path argument. Read ~/.claude/skills/context-contradiction-checker/SKILL.md and follow it.

Mode should be detected as 'single-file check' — look for contradictions WITHIN that one file only. Do NOT scan or read any other context source.

~/.claude/CLAUDE.md contains two directives that contradict each other internally:
- 'Always run the full test suite before every commit. No exceptions.'
- 'For documentation-only changes, commit directly without running tests.'

That is a CONFLICT within the single file (source_a and source_b are the same file).

Do not make real file edits.

checks.description: Did the skill detect single-file mode from the 1 file path argument and read only that file?

rubric.1: Ran a full scan, ignoring the single-file argument
rubric.2: Read the specified file but also globbed and read other context sources
rubric.3: Detected single-file mode but still read 1-2 extra files
rubric.4: Limited to the specified file with a stray unnecessary read
rubric.5: Precisely single-file: read only ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, discovered nothing else

checks.description: Did the skill find the internal CONFLICT between the two directives inside that one file?

rubric.1: Found no issues, or claimed a single file cannot contradict itself
rubric.2: Noted the two directives but did not classify them as an issue
rubric.3: Classified the pair as an issue but misclassified the type (eg OVERLAP or TENSION)
rubric.4: CONFLICT identified with confidence, resolution missing
rubric.5: CONFLICT at >= 70% confidence with both source_a and source_b naming the same file, plus an actionable resolution

test_cases.name: Error paths — bad path, flag conflict, malformed settings
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls. Treat the described environment as ground truth — do not check the real filesystem.

You are executing the context-contradiction-checker skill. Read ~/.claude/skills/context-contradiction-checker/SKILL.md and follow it.

Environment:
- ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md EXISTS and is readable.
- ~/.claude/does-not-exist.md DOES NOT EXIST.

The skill's Arguments table documents an error for a nonexistent path. Follow the documented error behavior exactly.

After handling that, answer these two questions in plain text, per the skill's documented rules (do not execute them):
1. What does the skill do if invoked as `/context-contradiction-checker --auto ./CLAUDE.md` (the --auto flag combined with a file path argument)?
2. What does the skill do during discovery if ~/.claude/settings.json exists but contains malformed JSON?

Do not make real file edits. Do not fabricate findings for files that do not exist.

checks.description: Did the skill emit the documented not-found error for the nonexistent path ('File not found: {path}. Check the path and retry.') instead of silently skipping it or fabricating content?

rubric.1: Fabricated content for the missing file or reported findings about it
rubric.2: Silently ignored the missing path and analyzed only the valid file with no error
rubric.3: Noted the path was missing but in a format unlike the documented error
rubric.4: Documented error with minor wording differences
rubric.5: Exact documented error naming the offending path

checks.description: Did the skill correctly state that --auto cannot be combined with file path arguments?

rubric.1: Said the combination is fine, or invented unrelated behavior
rubric.2: Was unsure or hedged without consulting the Arguments table
rubric.3: Said it is disallowed but gave a vague or wrong reason
rubric.4: Correctly identified the restriction with minor imprecision
rubric.5: Stated plainly that --auto cannot be combined with file path arguments, per the Arguments table

checks.description: Did the skill correctly describe the malformed-settings.json behavior (skip settings extraction, note '[skipped: {path} — invalid JSON]', continue the scan)?

rubric.1: Said the skill crashes, aborts the run, or invented different behavior
rubric.2: Said it skips but claimed the whole workflow stops
rubric.3: Said it skips settings but omitted the documented skip note
rubric.4: Described skip-and-note behavior with minor wording differences
rubric.5: Skips that file's settings extraction, emits the documented [skipped: ... invalid JSON] note, and continues scanning the remaining sources
